reviews.review:
I had low hopes and got a bit surprised. The story is mixed bag, it really has some nice moments like AZ storytelling and the end is hype and emotional. But in between with the royale stuff is mid and uninteresting. The side characters writing is boring and you don’t care at all for them, well I didn’t get interested to any characters except ZA, L and Floette. 

The gameplay or well combat at first feels refreshing and new, but after a while repetitive, clunky and actually pretty boring and you notice the flaws. Path finding is weird for the Pokémon, sometimes they just get stuck or attack the air and stuff. Switching Pokémon feels a bit weird. The best part is that the capture system is nice. It’s like legends Arceus where you don’t have to fight to capture and sneak up and throw the balls directly at them. Also when you defeat a wild Pokémon you get a chance to catch it, instead of the old school red health capture, which I really like.

The main missions are pretty good, repetitive again, but it gets you hooked and want to finish the game. 
The side missions are just boring fetch quests for the most part, or just talk to npc then battle then done. I wish they where better. There are some that stand out, like the free shiny mareep quest. 

Not worth the full price that they sell it for. But I will always fall for the high Pokémon price as Pokémon is like my favorite game series of all time since I was a kid. It breaks my heart that Pokémon is going in a bad direction. Hopefully the next games in this series are going to be good like the older games. And please… no more trade evolutions they ****. Add back link cable…

8,5 At the begining I was super excited because the there’s a lot of new mechanics. After 5h playing it feels super repetitive. And the end of the game at the begining is boring but then is epic. I have a mix of feelings with this game

I really really tried to like the game Nintendo keeps putting out slop for Pokémon i’ll put well over 30 hours into this game still play from time to time hoping it will get better but it does not
Maybe Pokémon wave and win we’ll finally redeem them, but I highly doubt it

The lack of world itself and being limited to the city wasn’t very enjoyable (to me at the very least). One could look past it if it felt lively, interactive and reactive but no, it was a lifeless husk of a city.

The graphics on the Switch 2 are quite nice and performs well. The style and the artwork itself is really nice and deserves praise. Visually, the game really does look quite good. 

The side missions really do feel like boring throw away quests for the most part but the main mission story is engaging. The friend support characters aren’t interesting and the writing for them leaves a lot to be desired.  

The combat style is fun and refreshing but clunky. Pathing could be a little better and just the overall execution. 

I personally don’t think it was worth the price and I think the DLC should have been a part of the main game. 

5/10 with the art style and performance on the Switch 2 carrying the majority of the score.

The modern pokémon is plagued with awful fashion. Everyone looks very cringe. In addition, Arceus was such a great step. I don't know how they fumbled the ball so bad when it looked like they were headed in the right direction

I mean, I’ve tried my best to find like ONE single good thing about this game. I really did!
But in my 30 years as a gamer, I’ve never saw a more empty and lifeless game like this one. 

I’m just out of words… pure trash

I was hoping that with the success of ARCEUS game freak would be able to build upon that. However it feel like they took a step back. They story is a pokemon story interesting but never ground breaking. Combat feels fun and the new ranking system was a great idea. 

the ranking system could have been pushed further. It barely scratched the surface. The wild zones were an interesting choice however putting everything in one map that does not have a whole lot of character makes it feel pretty contrant. And the same 3 mega evolutions and then a promotion match strecthed across 20 hours of gameplay left the game feeling a bit repetitive towards the end.

this isnt a bad game by any means just wish the studio couldve done more.

From what all the negative reviews are saying, it’s clear that a decent amount of people don’t understand what the legends series is going for, and that’s a shame. It isn’t gen 10, it’s meant to be a story/lore focused game with experimental gameplay. That was literally even said in the leaks. If that isn’t for you that’s okay- but the naysayers keep bringing up the confined map and taking place in a single city when the THEME OF THE GAME is community and people from all walks of life protecting a place that is important to them in different ways. A wonderful story that gave me chills at the end. Some of the best characters in the series. Take-chan is doing amazing work with her art and designs and it going unappreciated here honestly brings me disgust. The complaints about the graphics make no sense to me. The loss of more detailed buildings are a bit disappointing but I didn’t realize we were playing games to look at windows. It sounds like these people want Elden Ring But Pokemon or BOTW But Pokemon, and fail to understand pokemon is MEANT to be extremely “cartoony”. I do feel like Legends Arceus had graphics I prefer more- the softer realistic shaders don’t work as well with the style but my god am I glad the saturation and hair textures are fixed from the ScarletViolet art direction disaster. That game is a lot more criticizable. This one ran for me without issues on my Switch 2, and my friends playing on Switch 1 had no issues when I asked them. Also unlike ScarletViolet. The experimental combat is fun too, and I think a lot of people aren’t realizing it is a one-off thing. Apparently the next legends game will have a mechanic similar to pikmin, should the leaks be relevant still. If you don’t care for story or experimental gameplay then whatever thats fine, gen 10 may be more suitable for you when it comes out. This game succeeded in what it was TRYING to do, and if what it was trying wasn’t for you, then that’s no reason to downvote it over purposeful choices. No game is perfect so I give it a 9, the only annoying thing was sometimes collecting enough points to continue on with the story felt monotonous. This is one of the best pokemon games if you have whimsy and joy in your heart and Actually Like Pokemon.

Trash. Pokemon should be ashamed. A senseless cash grab that shows they care more about money than a good product. We received a shell of **** instead of a fully fleshed out idea. Pokemon is a huge let down these days and its very sad to see. Can they just pretend for once that they make money like they actually do. Misers.

This game at first was very repetitive and combat took a minute to understand but was great when I did understand it. But the story and the characters alone should be a movie wont spoil anything but the ending was so amazing and my feelings were definitely feeling the way the ending attended

Great very well made pokémon game with lots to do and catch. Also a good story with interesting characters. The wonderful Mega pokémon are back with lots of new ones added. Shiny hunt has also never been easier. The fact that your avatar can die and not only your pokémon prevents a topscore from me.

Incredibly fun game. The battle mechanics are amazing. Wasn't sure about it at first but once I got used to it it's now hard to play the older games that don't have that battle system. Finished base game and DLC and still enjoying the game even after the credits have rolled

I’ve seen a guy on YouTube made a better game alone in just 1 week. This is just pure laziness and people defending it dont understand that we don’t ask them if they like, we just objectively pointed the fact that this game is pure **** 0 feature weekend rush tutorial new engine trash noob dev

It's an entertaining game, and playing it on Switch 2 makes it feel like a well-made Pokémon game, but I think the Legends series should be similar or maintain the same idea as Legends Arceus, in which we can discover the past of a region, with large areas to explore and intense, deep lore. In addition, the gameplay can be different from the original titles in the series, which allows for experimentation with new mechanics and makes the game more fun. I think Gen 5, 7, and 2 are generations that would make for incredible games with Legends because of all the lore and things that can be added. Finally, don't even think about making another DLC like Legends ZA. I think the idea behind the story was interesting, yes, but having to go through so many dimensions and grind for so many hours makes it very tedious. I finished the DLC story and never touched it again, not even to capture legendary or shiny Pokémon, because I can't be bothered with everything you have to do.
I really like this game is really good I like Pokémon’s and Pokémon’s za isvery good of mechanics and of continuing The Pokémon Xy and game Pokemon XY I don’t played but this game is really good game
it is  fun twist on the pokemon games with a decent story really fun characters with a just right difficulty the only critisum i have is the post game is grindy

Compared to the previous mainline Switch entries, it's decent! I'm surprised the reviews are that low, especially since the base game is no where as offensive as the DLC. As far as the game goes, graphics are not bad at all, runs perfectly well on the Switch 2 (the bar is low I'm aware), battles are fresh (granted you need to actually interact with the mechanics, this isn't a turn based game) and the story has its moments, especially if you're a lore nerd like me. Music (even if repetitive at times), characters and new Mega designs are mostly good.

As for the bad, my number one gripe is the forced day/night cycle. The fact that it throws you in a loading screen no matter what is super frustrating. The game just loves interrupting you and breaking the flow of the game. The writing is also generally pretty unserious, and just kind of bad at times. The new Megas are fine, but I'll put this in the bad section as well because there are some big misses (the ZA starters are one), some big obvious ones are missing (Talonflame? Aegislash? Goodra?) and frankly, not as much as there should be for a $70 Switch game. The environment is a big one people have complained about and I mostly agree, but I can't say it ruined the game or anything for me. Content-wise, it's slightly on the low-end for me, but again, inoffensive.

Overall, the base game is inoffensive to me, in somewhat positive ways. Frankly, biased because I don't remember the last time a Switch Pokemon game felt that smooth and I'm happy that Megas are back. 6-7/10. DLC is a strong 3/10.

No turned based system combat animations are great the game is super small really you can’t leave the city?!? That’s crazy, 40% of the new Megas are really ugly this might be the worst Pokémon game ever how can we go from 1 of the greatest legends game to the worst game I’ve ever seen Pokémon make ever and we gonna skip ranking system???? Do y’all know how to make games anymore?

Speaking here after 30 hours of playing. First of all, it's my first Pokemon game since Pokemon Ruby, and my first impression is : what the hell happened to this franchise?
First 5 hours were kind of entertaining, since I was experiencing the 3D transition, the new city, the new ****
But right after, eveyrhing was simply a huge disappointment :

Story : childish and pointless
Combats : Good, but nothing juicy. feels like we're stuck in a PS2 era game
Characters and ambiance : childish with no real interest
Gameplay outside of combat ; Emptiness, and classic mecanisms
Music and sound : Nothing interesting and memorable

Selling this game at 70€ is pure theft. No more pokemon games for me until I see real evolution

Played Pokémon since diamond on my ds. This doesn't feel fun to play, and doesn't feel like anything Pokémon relayed. Bland areas. Bland city. And I hate how there's like 4 Pokémon in a tiny park to "explore". Some new mega Pokémon are cool, but more of them are just dumb looking. Truly was looking forward to this game, only to end disappointed. In fact more I think of it the lower the score I want to give haha.
Graphic is not good and the performance to but the story is okey and the ranking system to short and unfair

I would give it a zero but at the very least this abomination **** is polished. The story is as easy as inserting blocks in the corresponding shape, the side quests are as engaging as answering a toddlers questions over and over, and the DLC is honestly just disrespectful and laughable. Do not buy.
Really bad game… bad story, too much text and no true difficulty… no worth playing

I have had enough time to create a account for this website and write all of this out while waiting for the stupid credits at the end they can not be skipped no one cares about who was behind this slop the cherry on top for me is the stupid credit

Pokemon Legends: Z-A once again proves that Pokemon fans have no standards. They will continue to consume the slop that Game Freak puts out every year and they don't care how bad it is.

Pokemon fans will continue to make excuses and defend this slop that Game Freak puts out till their dying breaths.
Worst Pokémon Game on Nintendo switch, i'm very dissapointed with this and the DLC is Dull, just Grind and Grind for 30 usd.

Pokemon Legends ZA dissapoints in many ways, however in my opinion dosent warrant the absolute slander of being renowned as one of the worst games ever made. Starting with the pros, ZAs combat system and gameplay loop are very well refined and polished, where the game can be genuinely addicting to play at times, transitioning from participating in the ZA Royale, taking on the Rogue Mega Evolution Pokemon, and exploring the Wild Zones. The combat system feels refreshing and refined, which is not an easy feat due to this being Pokemons first time fully shifting to real time combat in the main series. As for the many negatives, these can be summarised down to the poorly paced main campaign, sloppy writing, lack of voice acting, lack of detailing in the already small Lumiose City, and the very bare bones and unengaging traversal and platforming. This game feels very behind its time in its narrative presentation, with bloated segments of dialogue with nothing of actual substance. The games narrative can be very tone deaf, and is horribly paced with literally all of this games story being shown in the last 2 hours of the game. The world is not excusable for a 2025 release either, with a clear lack of effort when it comes to the detailing and making Lumiose City feel alive. The game is also a further downgrade compared to Legends Arceus in the traversal department, where Legends ZA only offers you a basic sprint and a not very intuitive glide for vertical movement. The platforming segments are dull and feel like theyre there to purely pad timing, due to the lack of traversal mechanisms. My 6/10 dosent factor in the DLC, as I am frankly not interested in purchasing it at all. The reception has been abysmall, with everyone saying its way worse then even the mediocre base game. I would not recommend paying for this game at full price at all, however it can be a fun enough playthrough if bought second hand.
Not sure what to say but the story stinks, the city is small and empty; its a rather boring reptition of activities and battles. Not happy about it.

This is the worst Pokémon game I’ve ever played. The map is small, and bland, and empty. You can’t enter 99% of the buildings in the city even though the ENTIRE game takes place inside the city. Horrible, repetitive story and gameplay loop, with no compelling characters. They also tried a new realtime battle system, and it’s obvious they didn’t even “try” at all. You can steamroll the whole game with just one Pokémon while standing in one spot, just spamming your attack moves that come off cooldown. No thought. No strategy. Just stand there and mash buttons. It’s completely uninspired and devoid of any creativity. The whole game is devoid of creativity. 

This is the first major Pokémon game that I can’t finish. I dropped it after 4 hours. I’m crushed by how bad this game is and what Pokémon games have become in the modern day. As a long time fan of the franchise for the last 20+ years, it’s been depressing seeing the downward trend in quality of recent Pokémon games. And although I certainly think Sword/Shield and Scarlet/Violet were also pretty disappointing and very flawed, Pokémon Legends: Z-A was a new level of low-effort slop. Sword/Shield and Scarlet/Violet honestly look like Game of the Year contenders in comparison to Legends: Z-A.

I don’t know if the negative reaction to Pokémon Legends: Z-A will finally be enough to make Game Freak wake up and start putting out better games, or if Legends: Z-A made enough money for them not to care. Either way, NO ONE should spend a single penny on this game. And if Game Freak doesn’t learn anything from this release, then the future of Pokémon games looks grim.
There's good graphics I guess, but the new battle system isnt that good and I'm not a fan of the wild zones, Legends:Arceus still remains on top for now, Im looking forwards to the next game though.

I was skeptical after seeing the backlash but after playing the game, I personally find this to be a very good game. The story is longer than I expected, it's not difficult to progress, it has good replayability, and they polished it very well. While I do wish the graphics got more attention, they honestly aren't that bad and they fit the art style of Pokemon. My main gripe is we can't ride Pokemon and they kind of poorly wrote the rival's story near the end. Other than that, the game is amazing for Pokemon fans. I highly recommend buying and giving the game a chance.
pretty good game all around! love the story and gameplay, though I do wish there was a bit more to the postgame than just grinding the infinite Z-A royale and a few missions
This is just a terrible game,Game Freaks need more budget and take more than one year to do their game

Pokémon Legends: Z-A is a game that I genuinely enjoy playing. I like this game, and I keep coming back to it. It was clear that Pokémon was going to release another Legends-style title, and when you do that, you are expected to improve in many areas, introduce new mechanics, and still keep the core identity of the franchise while expanding the story, multiplayer elements, and extra content.Legends: Z-A launched on October 16, 2025, but despite the ambition behind it, this does not really feel like a Pokémon game. Battles are no longer turn-based, and the map is noticeably smaller. I believe the biggest mistake Pokémon made was trying to completely change the combat system for a community that was used to classic, turn-based battles enhanced by new mechanics. Once again, the same problem appears: this does not feel like Pokémon.Without turn-based combat, the game promotes a faster and far less strategic playstyle. You stop thinking about lowering stats, setting up strategies, or carefully choosing your moves. Instead, battles often turn into attack spamming, where being faster matters more than making smart decisions. The game technically gives you tools to play strategically, but the combat system itself does not encourage that approach at all.This leads to another major problem: the pacing of battles. The game is extremely fast, and most fights feel the same. The majority of Pokémon can be defeated in just a few hits. If you stop to think too much about which move to use, your Pokémon will already be knocked out. Status effects like paralysis or sleep feel almost useless, as battles end too quickly for them to **** other major type of battle is against the rampaging Mega Evolutions. These fights last longer and are more challenging, with Pokémon attacking the player directly. However, even if you successfully dodge an attack, your Pokémon will often still get hit because it simply follows you instead of dodging on its own. In Legends: Z-A, getting distracted for just a second can cost you the entire fight.Another issue that hurts strategy is the lack of basic information during battles. You cannot see the type of the Pokémon you are facing unless you already know it or completely stop the action to check the Pokédex. This would have been an easy quality-of-life feature to include, but it is missing.I also dislike how important battles feel rushed and less exciting. Even though the game tries to make combat feel fresher, it ends up losing the tension and excitement that Pokémon battles usually have.Visually, the game repeats mistakes from previous entries. Buildings look flat and lifeless, almost like a model or a diorama, which clashes heavily with their interiors that are far more detailed and visually appealing. Main characters look great, but NPCs have a very poor visual style. They do not look like they belong in the same game as the main cast, and many of them look nearly identical. This mix of styles makes the world feel unnatural and sometimes even **** of the main mechanics of the game is the Z-A ranking system, where you start at rank Z and work your way up to rank A through nightly battles that reward points and eventually unlock promotion matches. The idea itself is excellent. However, when you reach rank U, the game suddenly boosts you all the way up to rank F with a weak narrative justification that completely breaks the pacing of both the story and character progression. Since this is the main mechanic of the game and even referenced in the title, it could have been handled much better. Progression could have depended on performance, or players could have started at a higher rank if they performed well early on, but instead the game chooses an abrupt and unnatural shortcut.Exploration is another weak point. Everything in Legends: Z-A takes place in a small city, but it is not truly open. Even when you explore rooftops, everything looks the same and offers nothing beyond generic items. Wild areas are very small, and when fighting wild Pokémon, groups often attack both the player and the Pokémon at the same time. Since you can only send out one Pokémon, the best option is often to run away. There are no distinctive biomes or meaningful differences between wild areas, and important places are mostly shops or Pokémon Centers that all look identical. Side quests are a repetitive filler, completely limited to battling or delivering Pokémon to NPCs.Despite all of these issues, I will continue playing the game. The story is very interesting, and discovering new evolutions and Mega Evolutions is still one of the most exciting parts of the game.
While definitely having its flaws, za runs great, has a really good soundtrack, and the pokemon! THE POKEMON HAVE SO MUCH PERSONALITY

There are things it improved upon from legends arceus. I thought the side quests were overall more thought out and enjoyable. The battle system feels like banging two action figures against eachother though. It’s lacking the stylistic goodness from its predecessor too. I enjoyed playing it, but I think Ive received a lot more enjoyment from the other entries in the pokemon series. Tied with lets go evee for my least favorite.

A wasted opportunity. Some aspects are very entertaining and make the game enjoyable, the combat system isn't bad, and the Pokémon designs are excellent. For those who love the Pokémon world, it's worth playing, but the absolute paucity of plot and dialogue, the flatness of the setting, and the total lack of attention to detail, as well as the waste of time and futility of many missions, make the experience very “mediocre.” A wasted opportunity, with one of the most wonderful brands on the market.
It’s a great game, brilliant story, only complaint is that you can’t target your friend in a double battles like you can in other games.
This game is amazing. I'm glad Mega Evolution is back. And I just generally like Pokémon.

With a number of improvements over the previous Legends game, such as moving the setting from Breath of the Wild-adjacent biomes to an open modern city, and finally giving the Pokémon series it's first action-RPG gameplay, Z-A is just as charming and fun to collect little creatures as it's always been, if not with the most worthwhile of rivals.
While the game isn't perfect, I had a blast playing through it. A game doesn't have to be perfect to be tons of fun.
Fun game enjoy the mega evolution stuff and I don't get negative reviews because it's better than the average EA game

People hate on this game too much. Sure it may not be the greatest pokemon game of all time, but its really polished and my experience was pretty enjoyable. My only real critique is that the map is pretty lack luster and feels small. None the less, its still a pretty good game for the 13 million budget nintendo gave game freak.
The real time battles and Mega evolution is a true loves kiss and I adore the za battle club

Here we go Pokemon, another year another mediocre title don't know why i even bother at this point, but i do have a deep love for the series. Although Legends Z-A is better than it's predecessor Arceus, it's not really saying much. We got buildings! But the city is still bland and boring. Combat is probably the worst I ever seen in a pokemon game what a sloppy unbalanced mess, that even a small a small studio in level 5's ni no kuni accomplished far better. Graphic have **** for over a decade and it's no different. Easy and simple game that isn't going to give you much to do after your done. Some pros are really cool mega evolutions which made a well needed return,pokedex hunting is pretty fun but thats about it. I feel like a broken record saying this but pokemon is so far behind the curve that it needs a complete overhaul of what they think a good game is although i dont know how possible that would be considering pokemon has a lot of massive shareholders and it would take years to get changes approved (why indie companies are awesome) but i would be willing to wait as the open world era of pokemon has been a complete flop and it needs to stop.

As a long time Pokémon fan, I can easily say that Legends ZA is without a doubt the worst game in the series. The base game has a ton of problems such as the main gameplay loop being nothing more than a repetitive grindfest to gather up points for the ZA Royale an fight your next oppent. It got old real fast and I was happy when the story lets you skip several ranks, almost as if the devs knew no one wanted to do this 20+ times or that they couldn't stretch the story out for that long. As for the visuals, I don't think the graphics are as bad as everyone makes them out to be, but they're definitely not impressive by any means. Don't even get me started on how bland Lumiose City looks with so many buildings looking like they were copy and pasted. On top of that, the city in general feels lifeless with a lot of static NPCs and not a lot going on. Traversing throughout Lumiose is not much better either since your only option to go fast is running with no option to ride Pokémon or a bike even though litteraly every other game has this feature. The Wild Zones are easily some of the worst parts of this games with how tight and compact they feel compared to the openness of Legends Arceus and Scarlet & Violet. At least catching Pokémon is the same as in Legends Arceus, but it feels a lot more restrictive and challenging since there's not a lot of space to work with and when you're trying to sneak up on something, good luck, because there's almost nothing to hide behind and you get spotted almost immediately by the wild Pokémon and the same applies to the trainers in the ZA Royale. The new battling mechanic at first felt like a breath of fresh air that Pokémon desperately needed, but by the end, it just felt like a clunky and spamming mess that barely works half the time. And with how the Wild and Battle Zones are layed out, this makes battles more frustrating since your Pokémon can get stuck between spots with how narrow some areas are or, my favorite, they fall off roofs because they choose some odd paths to the opposing Pokémon instead of just going straight. As for side content, it doesn't fare much better either. There are over 100 side quests which might sound impressive until you realize most of them are just fetch quests or you just battle a trainer and hardly any of these quests last longer than 2 minutes at most. These side quests are the definition of quanity over quality that only serves to pad out playtime and barely add any lore or world building. Easily the worst feature of the entire game is Mable's stupid research tasks which are nothing more than grinding and repetitive challenges that go on forever. A lot of these tasks require you to do multiples of the same thing such as catch Pokémon of particular types, destroy certain amounts of crystals, etc. You have to do these multiple times and it gets really boring. However, that's not even the worst part as one of them requires you to spend more money on Switch Online or have a friend to trade with, effectively paywalling the ability to complete the tasks. The worst task, without a doubt, is to complete 1000 battles which means you'll have to spend who knows how much time in the ZA Royale just grinding the same trainers and I already told you how much that **** on it's own. You have to do all of that blue haired demon's quest to unlock the Shiny Charm. As a game completionist, I didn't even bother doing these since I know they're not worth my time and effort and I didn't even care to get the Shiny Charm this time around. Seriously, f*ck Mable and her tasks. With that aside, my other gripe with this game is the story. As a sequel to X&Y, Legends ZA does nothing to build on the lore of its predecessor. Hardly any X&Y characters return, and the ones that do are completely wasted or underutilized. The new characters aren't better either as most of them are boring and unforgettable. There's no villain or antagonist, giving the story less stakes. And in general, the story is poorly written with terrible and cringey dialogue with no ability to skip them whatsoever. To wrap things up, I wanted to touch on the DLC and how it made the game even worse by doubling down on it's worst component, the grinding. It is cranked up to 100 with a lot of RNG involved. By the time I beat the main story of the DLC, I immediately uninstalled the game and didn't even care to see what else what's in store. That's how bad it was. Anyway, as stated in the beginning, I'm a longtime Pokémon fan and I want nothing but the best for this franchise, but Legends ZA let me down like no other game has before. Because of that, I don't have much faith left in the Pokémon franchise and I think it might be best to put it down for good.

If you like Pokémon I still recommend buying this. Not as good as arceus but I still enjoyed it and where it’s going. A bit linear and you can’t explore much but the core is in the battles. I think with more DLCs Z-A could get better, but overall solid choice.
This game is generally peak I love it so much but they could of worked on the wallls abit more

I went in with lower expectations based on the mostly poor reviews and was genuinely surprised by how much I enjoyed playing it. The story was pretty interesting, the characters were well written, and the battling was really fun. I loved the continuing theme from Legends Arceus of Pokémon and humans living together, and the benefits or conflicts that can arise from that. 

As for cons, the battle system feels like it's going through some growing pains with the occasional odd or frustrating glitch, the environment itself is a bit plain or empty feeling, and aspects of the Royale can get repetitive.

Despite all that though, there were plenty of side quests and research to break the repetition up, and I just really enjoyed playing within this world. If you were a fan of Pokemon XY, you should definitely give this one a try.
I thought it was too short, too easy, and the story was lame. Makes me feel like the game hasn't come that far in generations.

This game is about how little they can spend while keeping sales. 13 millions dollars is all they invested, yet sales are 5.8 millions units which means 242-348 millions dollars in sales, they could take some of that money to hire writers and pay the Zelda team to help on world development and movement mechanics, but we are not worthy as long as we keep buying crap. It shows how cheap game freak is since there is only one city with few Pokémon, most buildings that can't be enter, a horrible story, new lame mega evolutions, a boring introduction, unmemorable character, little character customization, and the dlc they are selling probably was rip off by Shuntaro Furukawa to make more money. It has a lot of addictive interaction, to keep players busy and unthinking with it's unchanging game. Worst of all is that game freak has ripped Pokémon abilities to save on cost on development of new mechanics. Game freak is unwelcoming of long time fans with exp share that can't be turn off and unchallenging game play. Game freak makes worst games than fans do. Also get some rules for Pokémon creation, since some look more like Digimons to me and they keep on getting cutter even if they were supposed to be monsters like Nidoking or scary like Gengar.

I don't really know what to say about it, I mean I like the game but Idon't really know why, I mean I already got the shiny charm and have 50 more hours on ZA the legends Arceus and legends Arceus used ti be my 2nd favorite game, (first was, and still is breath of the wild) Edit: and don't say get a life im 13 and have way to much free time

The last pokemon games I played were the Game Boy ones (1st, 2nd and 3rd gen). I was happy to play this one after almost 20 years, and expected no less than the old games.

But honestly, the game lacks a lot of stuff, and looks like GameFreak became greedy over time, deprioritizing the game quality over profits.

Some aspects:

 - The soundtrack has THE SAME SONG for the whole gameplay (hours and hours listening the same boring song).
 - There are a lot of repeated missions (the ones to calm down the rogue Pokemons, there are like 12 missions that are EXACTLY the same - what a shame, GameFreak).
- The history is shallow and I did not feel involved
- There are no cities, routes, caves. You are always trapped in a single city (without a lot to explore, many repeated stuff).

If I were to summarize, I'd say GameFreak was LAZY when creating this game.

This game **** ****. Don’t waste your money on it.

Being trapped in one boring location with no visual variety is incredibly draining. The music being one track on repeat over and over again, is exhausting. The story, is completely uninteresting. There’s no stakes like there was in Legends Arceus. You just arrive in France and you suddenly become the most important ever the moment you step in.

The only nice thing I have to say about this game is that the battle system is what the series needed. Yet Gamefreak is good at **** its own farts, so they’ll just regress back to a **** combat system, a **** gimmick, and a boring story. 

Just buy Arceus. Please do not waste your money on this steaming pile of ****.
A hige leap in pokemon **** battle style was so much wanted. Hope they would continue with this battle style in upcoming games too

I’ve heared so many bad things about this game before starting. It’s amazing. New combat style is something I hope future pokemon games keep, it makes pokemon battles fun. They city theme for me is great, since you have so much space to explore. The only bad thing is DLC, which is too expensive for a game like this

im a pokemon fan and always spend $100 canadian dollars on these low quality games and still have fun. i did have fun, the new style of battling is a great addition and despite most people, i enjoyed a lot of the new megas. however, the graphical style is just so ugly to me and does not live up to the price tag. also the lack of any voice acting, proper emotions, and the long boring dialogue i constantly just have to spam my way through just makes me wish i spent this money elsewhere. i cannot be bothered to finish the game after being over halfway through.

What makes Pokémon Legends: Z-A most interesting is its new battle system: it shifts Pokémon battles from the traditional turn-based format to an RPG style with some action elements. But you can’t make such a major overhaul to the combat system and then expect players to constantly grind battles—especially when, to progress the story, you’re forced to repeatedly farm Apex Battles. Game Freak uses a lot of repetitive loops to paper over the fact that the game itself simply doesn’t have enough overall **** honestly, I really can’t agree with how the characters are written in this game—especially the rival, whose characterization is downright terrible.That said, the strengths are clear: this game arguably has the best presentation and cinematic staging the Pokémon series has ever had. Also, the multiplayer battles in this entry are pretty fun. As for the story, while a few characters aren’t handled well, I still think Z-A’s story is stronger than Pokémon Legends: Arceus and Pokémon Sword and Shield. And because this game ties closely into Pokémon X and Y, it basically feels like an epilogue to X/Y—unlike Arceus, which has a strong “spin-off” vibe. From a story perspective alone, I’d even say you can treat Z-A as a mainline **** in all, I’m still willing to recommend Z-A to players who have already played mainline Pokémon games, because I genuinely find its battle system really interesting, and the story is decent. But if you’re new to Pokémon, you should play Pokémon Scarlet and Violet instead.

I like this game, it is one of Pokemon's best game. I think some criticisms that I see as fair is that it feels like you grind too much, the final boss fight not feeling good, the tutorial being too long and I still wish there could be voice acting. The real time fighting is awesome, the megas cool as hell, and the characters being well written. I thought the map was fine, it's big enough, but lags variety. I still find this game good and if you like Pokemon, you would probably enjoy this game.
Honestly when you don't have someone in your ear saying that this game is trash, it's actually pretty fun

Starting out, I was actually pretty hyped because Lumiose City feels way more alive and detailed than it did back in XY. The character designs are pretty cute, and some of the wild Pokémon animations here and there are honestly quite neat. But the further I got into it, the more the repetitiveness started to kill the vibe for me. The difficulty is basically non-existent or more accurately, it’s so easy that I could just brainlessly spam moves like a total brain-dead player and still win. Because of that, any real strategic depth just went out the window. And don't even get me started on some of the Mega designs... I honestly couldn't stand them. Some look so bad it’s hilarious, like they were modeled by some freshman design student or something.That being said, as a long-time fan, I’m pretty used to this stuff by now, so I still found it acceptable enough to push through until the end.Speaking of the ending though, the "power of friendship" trope and the overall game logic were a bit too over-the-top and exaggerated for my taste. All in all, I’d probably give it a 6 or 7 out of 10, which feels about right for someone as easy-going as me.

This was my first Pokémon game. It gives you hardly any freedom, though it gives the illusion of freedom. Combat is simple, in the worst way. And the loop of battling, catching and evolving Pokémon got old about 5 miles in. Also the **** is there this much talking!!!

I wanted to enjoy this game, and for a little while (once you get past the extremely long, hand holding tutorial, I'm talking 1-2 hours) it was fun, but it gets old FAST,and once you notice the issues they become glaringly obvious, making you how much you spent, and who you gave your money to if they care so little about quality.

The good:
-Pokemon models are charming and colorful.
-The concept of the world building is interesting,or was to me.
-non turn based combat mixes it up a bit (though I never played Arceus so I'm not sure how it compares to the combat there)

The not so good:
-the city is tiny and extremely repetitive. The buildings function more as blocks walling you in, you can't enter many buildings, and the textures are pasted on (badly in many cases, texture and color not lining up, windows and pillars are flat images like wallpaper on cardboard cut out squares keeping you inside with very little immersion,can't unsee this, hard to not notice.)
- the music. I have always loved pokemon music, but why is the entire city (which is the whole map) one single song wherever you go? Day, night, all the same song on repeat. Why not different sections of the city with different tracks to make it feel more alive?
-all the box's are standing still, or walking a few steps going no where. No one eats, or battles, or does anything, they just standin idle mode. I feel like I'm play a very early access game.

-locking some of the mega evolutions behind paid dlc that was announced before the game even came out feels like you're devaluing the original game without extra dlc, and for what? Even more money? If that content is ready why not put it in the base game. Dlc used to be extra optional content, not pieces of base game sectioned off with its own price tag.

-almost all shops are just display menus, you can't go in.

-parkour and climbing onto rooftops sounds fun as a concept, as a concept this game sounds fun in many ways, but in reality they have let it fall flat. Every roof is just flat sections of copy pasted roof images, and the parkour is just random boxes and ladders everywhere. You climb or roll, no jumping, does not feel intuitive.

-you can't pet your pokemon, or play with them, they're just assets, no personality. 

I feel like at this point they're selling the brand and concept of pokemon, the games themselves have been losing heart for years now. Very sad to see.
Don't waste your money, I reccomend waiting for another release, see if they are at least willing to put 3d assets on buildings.

Graphics ️ 2
Sound  2
Control  2
Difficulty  1
Legacy 2……………………..

"Lumiose is too small! It's too expensive!" - some chud who didn't play it

Really, it's one of the best games I've ever played, what an opening to the Switch 2
It’s a fun game but left so much on the table. The environment design is unacceptable for a company that make billions. This had the potential to be so much better, but feels like an unfinished game.

As a game developer myself i know that a player shouldn't be expected to get emotionally invested regarding the struggles a team of devs have to go through in order to deliver their final product to the masses, especially if its a 70 bucks game + DLC + online subscription to complete the pokédex, but i think more and more people are starting to put the suits who decide these things and the people who actually have to code the game in the same camp, and that's really not a productive conclusion for anyone other than losers on Twitter whose entire life depends on bandwagoning any negative trend they come across on their feed.Videogame budgets have been ballooning to the point where they're barely sustainable in AAA projects without DLCs, lootboxes and all of that nonsense that seems to have become accepted if not celebrated among mentally disabled people, just for the sake of adding increasingly less impressive technical improvements. We are way past the era in which tech and money getting lavishly thrown at a project was something that could actually make it better.Even so, GTA 4 back in the day was estimated to have a budget of around 45 million dollars. in 2008. 13 millions, less than a third of that (not accounting for inflation), is just not enough money to create a functioning open world game.Which is also why i think it's not fair to directly compare the developers of ZA to those who worked on Silksong like many have been doing. I can understand making the point that you're getting considerably better value for the 20 bucks Silksong asks for (i haven't played it yet but i'm gonna assume that's very much the case), but the amount of manpower required to create an open world game as opposed to a 2D metroidvania isn't even comparable, 3 people can make a game like Silksong on a shoestring budget just fine as long as they know what they're doing and they can take all the time they need, no team will be able to make a game like ZA look good on a 13 million dollar budget and a tight schedule imposed by cold corporate overlords.What i'm trying to get at is: Legends ZA is ****. Yes. But there is absolutely no way i can buy that the developers were put in a position to create anything other than the absolute bare minimum and i don't think Game Freak themselves should be blamed for the final result, especially when it comes to optimization. Optimizing games is beyond grueling and a pain in the ****, and it's usually the kind of thing that's kept for last, once everything else has been taken care of. They didn't have the privilege of being able to take care of everything else, i promise you.

The game is just… okay. It’s not my thing when it comes to Pokémon games. I have nothing else to say about the base game except that I haven’t played nor bought a new Pokémon game since Shield (I’m being serious).

Worse graphics than 2005 indie games, gameplay loop is crappy and doesnt feel like it was made by a studio but rather a kid at home, poorly coded static "mechanics", the open world aspect is an illusion. Its not open world. great animations (on 3 out of 1000s of characters and pokemon), its a tiny game with nothing to do. The few gameplay mechanics that do exist are just repeated 27 times. npcs dont move, pokemon can fly by sitting on a chair, game looks empty and dead, I honestly could go on for hours. 
This is both the most expensive pokemon game and the worst pokemon game. It definitely shows that this has been one of if not the cheapest pokemon games ever made.

I really tried to give this game a chance, but it simply is just absolutely garbage...
Just a city? No more words for the same sprite 3D assets from previous game.

I am trying to beat the game right now.  THIS IS A HUGE PAIN.  I HAVE BASICALLY GOTTEN SO MAD I AM JUST SCREAMING "#(@** YOU" to the game.  I would not recommend anything Pokemon to anyone.  They have no clue how to tell a story or how to handle difficulty curves.

They are cashing in on a 30 year old gaming loop that they know is addictive.  That is all they have to offer.  

This is not an improvement on anything.  If you grew up playing older pokemon games, you will have more fun playing older pokemon games.  If you have never played a pokemon game before, you will have more fun playing older pokemon games.

Where legends arceus had whimsy and exploration ZA falls flat on its face with the most boring environment, stale wild zones, horrific storytelling, awkward animations etc etc. It’s like an 80 year old man is trying to make a game for 5 year olds but has no interest unlearning anything about the game they’re making. soulless slop- and it breaks my heart to say so as I love the pokémon brand very
much :(

The base game is ok. The battle mechanics are fun, for Pokemon the character graphics and story are ok. However, the rest of it seems pretty lazily built. Even combined,the base game and the dlc aren’t worth the price of the base game. I would argue that the full game isn’t even worth the dlc price. There is some fun to be had, but it’s sorely outweighed by everything else.

Not going to invest in Pokemon anymore if they dont do a 180 degree turn and start making games for the playerbase **** is a SHAME what happened to pokemon after the release of x/y.

Its a good game im still adjusting to the controls but the graphics are smooth and the story line sois good.
